brief_document.title: Aion event WebSocket manager with reconnect and resync
brief_document.purpose: Provide the live spine of the dashboard: a singleton WebSocket manager that subscribes to the server's event stream, parses typed events, and survives network drops with bounded reconnect and active-view resync.
brief_document.task: Build a singleton WebSocket manager modelled on apps/web's, specialised for the Aion event protocol: subscribe by filter (per-workflow, filtered, firehose), parse incoming frames into typed Event values, dispatch to handlers, reconnect with bounded backoff on drop, re-establish the active subscription and trigger a resync, and expose connection status to the UI.

requirements.title: Singleton manager and subscription protocol
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement a singleton WebSocket manager that connects to the server's event endpoint and supports subscribe-by-filter for per-workflow, filtered, and firehose subscriptions, parsing incoming frames into typed Event values and dispatching them to registered handlers.
acceptance: src/lib/api/websocket.ts defines a singleton manager modelled on apps/web's (handler set, connect(), close(), subscribe()/unsubscribe()).
subscribe accepts a filter descriptor (per-workflow id, filtered by type/status/namespace, or firehose) and registers a handler; the manager sends the AW subscribe message for that filter.
Incoming frames are JSON-parsed into typed Event values and dispatched to the matching handlers; parse failures are logged, not thrown into the UI.
The subscribe-message shape and the event-frame shape are isolated in this module as the AW protocol surface (single place to update when AW pins it).
A unit test feeds synthetic frames through a mocked socket and asserts handlers receive typed events.

requirements.title: Bounded reconnect and resync on drop
requirements.spec: WHEN the socket disconnects, THE SYSTEM SHALL reconnect with bounded backoff, re-establish the active subscription on reconnect, and signal a resync so the active view can re-fetch or replay after its last-seen sequence. THE SYSTEM SHALL notify connect/disconnect handlers throughout.
acceptance: On unexpected close the manager retries with bounded backoff up to a configured attempt ceiling, then surfaces a disconnected state (no infinite tight-loop reconnect).
On successful reconnect the manager re-sends the active subscription message and fires a resync notification to subscribers.
Subscribers can supply a last-seen sequence so resync replays after_seq where the AW protocol supports it; otherwise resync falls back to a full re-fetch signal.
connect and disconnect handler sets are notified on each transition.
A unit test simulates a drop and asserts reconnect is attempted, the subscription is re-sent, and a resync is signalled.

requirements.title: Connection status hook
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL expose a useConnectionStatus hook returning the live connection state (connected, reconnecting, disconnected) for the UI to render an indicator.
acceptance: src/features/live-feed/hooks/useConnectionStatus.ts subscribes to the manager's connect/disconnect transitions and returns a connected | reconnecting | disconnected state.
The hook cleans up its subscription on unmount.
A test drives the manager through connect → drop → reconnect and asserts the hook reports the matching state sequence.

boundaries: SHALL NOT define the WebSocket endpoint or protocol — that is cluster AW; this brief implements a client for it.
SHALL NOT wire subscriptions into the list/detail views — that is AU-009.
SHALL NOT render the connection indicator component — that is AU-008 (this brief provides the status hook it consumes).
verification: bun test passes for the manager and hook unit tests.
No lint-disable comments added; reconnect is bounded (no infinite tight loop).

checklist.text: A singleton WebSocket manager connects to the server's event stream and exposes subscribe-by-filter for per-workflow, filtered, and firehose subscriptions.
The manager parses incoming frames into typed Event values and dispatches them to registered handlers.
On disconnect the manager reconnects with bounded backoff and notifies connect/disconnect handlers driving a connection indicator.
On reconnect the active subscription is re-established and a resync is triggered (re-fetch or replay after last-seen sequence where the protocol supports it).
A useConnectionStatus hook exposes connected/reconnecting/disconnected state for the UI.

stories.text: As an on-call operator, I want a persistent connection indicator and automatic reconnection so that I always know whether what I am looking at is current, and the view resumes after a network blip.

constraints.text: Match the apps/web stack exactly: React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind v4, Bun, Biome (100-char), shadcn/Radix primitives, TanStack Query. No new framework, styling system, or state library.

Wire types come from the house generated-types pipeline (Rust #[derive(TS)] → src/types/generated/). The dashboard never hand-defines a type aion-core/aion-proto owns and never hand-edits a generated file.

The dashboard consumes the AW API/WebSocket contract; it defines no server endpoint, no WebSocket protocol, and no asset-hosting mechanism. Not-yet-pinned contract details are isolated in the api module for a one-file change.
Feature-folder convention from apps/web: each feature is components/ + hooks/ (+ context/lib as needed) + types.ts + index.ts. Barrels re-export; logic lives in named files.
Server state goes through TanStack Query; live updates flow through the WebSocket manager and patch query caches or local view state. No bespoke fetch-into-useState for server data.
Every async surface renders explicit loading, empty, and error states. No surface spins indefinitely or blanks on failure.

The WebSocket manager reconnects with bounded backoff on drop and the active view resyncs (re-fetch history/query, or replay after last-seen sequence where supported). A connection indicator is always present; a dropped socket never leaves a silently stale view.
The event timeline is ordered strictly by event sequence number and de-duplicates events arriving via both history fetch and live stream.
Biome lint + format clean (bun run check) and tsc --noEmit clean. No lint-disable comments to silence findings.
Namespace selection scopes every query and every subscription; switching namespace re-scopes both. No unscoped query when a namespace is selected.

resolved_context.intention: Build the human-facing operational web UI for aion-server: a browser application that lets an operator see which workflows are running and which have failed, drill into a single execution's full event history rendered as a timeline, and watch events stream live as they happen. This cluster builds the frontend only. It consumes the HTTP/gRPC list/query/history API and the WebSocket event stream that the server (cluster AW) exposes and hosts as static assets; it does not run the engine, persist anything, or define the API. It must match the existing house frontend stack (React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind v4 + Biome + Bun, shadcn/Radix, TanStack Query, a singleton WebSocket manager with reconnect, generated types from Rust) exactly, so it reads as part of the same codebase as apps/web. When done, an operator pointed at a running aion-server can observe every workflow in every namespace, drill into any execution's full history, and watch events stream in real time — with production-grade loading, empty, error, and reconnection behaviour throughout.

provenance.quote: Build the human-facing operational web UI for aion-server: a browser application that lets an operator see which workflows are running and which have failed, drill into a single execution's full event
